input.headline: Ní thuigim: does AI even understand Gaeilge?

raw.headline: Ní thuigim: does AI even understand Gaeilge?

winner: AI learns 50 languages and finally masters Gaeilge—its translation of "sláinte" is "salad.

candidates.text: Why did the AI refuse to translate from Gaeilge? Because it couldn't *un-derstand* a language!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'understand' and 'un-derstand' are clever and specific to the headline, with a concise and surprising twist."}

candidates.text: AI finally learned Irish, so I tried teaching it Gaelic swearing—it just said "hello" and gave me a 404 error code.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with tech jargon, specific to AI and language learning, concise and funny."}

candidates.text: What did the AI say when it tried to learn Irish? "I don't understand, I only know how to generate nonsense in 50 languages, none of which is Gaeilge.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, specific, and surprising with a clever twist on AI's limitations. It uses natural English and effectively ties the headline to the punchline."}

candidates.text: Why did the AI try to learn Irish? It kept getting lost in translation—literally!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat relevant and uses a play on words with 'translation' and 'lost,' but it lacks specificity to the headline's focus on AI understanding Gaeilge. It's mildly funny but could be more precise and surprising."}
